Imports in scope: LeanSrc.Examples.

### Input

* `<hint_lemmas>`: Admitted lemmas available in scope. You may use any of them in your fix.

<local_ctx>
def drop (n: Nat) (xs: List α) : List α :=
  match n, xs with
  | 0, _ => xs
  | _, [] => []
  | n + 1, _ :: xs => drop n xs

def zip' : List α → List β → List (α × β)
  | [], _ => []
  | _, [] => []
  | x::xs, y::ys => ⟨x, y⟩ :: zip' xs ys
</local_ctx>

<broken_theorem>
theorem (n: Nat) (xs: List α) (ys: List β) : (drop n (zip' xs ys) = zip' (drop n xs) (drop n ys)) := by
  induction n with
    | zero =>
        intro xs ys
        simp [drop]
    | succ n ih =>
        intro xs ys
        cases xs with
        | nil =>
            simp [drop, zip']
        | cons x xs =>
            cases ys with
            | nil =>
                simp [drop, zip']
            | cons y ys =>
                simpa using lemma_hint_6 (x := x) (y := y) (xs := xs) (ys := ys) (n := n) (h1 := ih xs ys)
</broken_theorem>

After `induction n with`, the variables `xs ys` are already in the context (they are not generalized), so `intro xs ys` fails because there are no binders left to introduce. Remove those `intro`s. In the successor case, use the induction hypothesis `ih` directly when applying `lemma_hint_6`.
